mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weak aggregate demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly supports corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline by more than 1% annualized or yields negative returns in more than 45% of co-occurring months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term equity price appreciation over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations during early-cycle phases or loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 0.4% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of high-inflation months or the signal Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5 in walk-forward validation tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative returns in more than 40% of triggered months or fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over rolling 2-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend levels indicates strong macroeconomic momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDP signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of quarters or exhibits a rank correlation below 0.05 with subsequent equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals contracting manufacturing activity and weakening economic momentum, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample validation.
